text:
Zhikun Lu
Zhikun Lu is an Assistant Professor of Operations and Business Analytics at NYU Shanghai. He received his PhD in Business from Emory University’s Goizueta Business School. His research focuses on causality-driven decision making, helping businesses ground their decisions in empirical evidence and causal reasoning. He brings this approach into practice through collaborations with leading tech companies such as Alibaba, Amazon, DiDi, and Tencent, translating research insights into practical strategies that generate real-world impact.
Select Publications
"The Value of Last-mile Delivery in Online Retail" with Ruomeng Cui, Tianshu Sun, and Lixia Wu, forthcoming, Management Science
"A Model of China's Economic Vertical Structure" with Xi Li, Xuewen Liu, and Yong Wang, forthcoming, Journal of Public Economics
"Preferential Credit Policy with Sectoral Markup Heterogeneity" with Kaiji Chen, Yuxuan Huang, Xuewen Liu, and Yong Wang, forthcoming, Journal of International Economics
"Sooner or Later? Promising Delivery Speed in Online Retail," with Ruomeng Cui, Tianshu Sun, and Joe Golden, 2024, Manufacturing & Service Operations Management
"How Contagious Was the Panic of 1907? New Evidence from Trust Company Stocks," with Caroline Fohlin, 2021, AEA Papers and Proceedings
Education
Ph.D., Business (Information Systems & Operations Management)Emory University
Digital Platforms
Online Retail
Logistics and Supply Chains

text_blocks.text: Zhikun Lu is an Assistant Professor of Operations and Business Analytics at NYU Shanghai. He received his PhD in Business from Emory University’s Goizueta Business School. His research focuses on causality-driven decision making, helping businesses ground their decisions in empirical evidence and causal reasoning. He brings this approach into practice through collaborations with leading tech companies such as Alibaba, Amazon, DiDi, and Tencent, translating research insights into practical strategies that generate real-world impact.
